x-enumeration-literals.name: Cash Payment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The chargeback amount is paid directly to the customer in cash or via bank transfer.

Comment:
Example: €100 refunded to the customers bank account.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Account Credit Payment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The refunded amount is credited to the customers account for immediate use.

Comment:
Example: Cashback or chargeback credited to a current or card account balance.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Statement Offset Payment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The chargeback is applied as a credit against an outstanding statement or balance.

Comment:
Example: €50 chargeback applied to reduce a credit card statement.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Voucher Payment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The refunded value is delivered as a voucher, coupon, or gift card instead of cash.

Comment:
Example: €30 refunded as a store voucher.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Merchant Settlement Payment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The chargeback is settled via a third party or merchant system rather than directly to the customer.

Comment:
Example: Payment processor reverses the transaction to the merchant, and the merchant refunds the customer.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Partial Payment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Only part of the chargeback amount is delivered via this payment method.

Comment:
Example: €60 refunded now, remaining €40 pending verification.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Scheduled Payment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Chargeback is delivered at a later date or scheduled event.

Comment:
Example: Refund applied at the next billing cycle.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Hybrid Payment
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Combination of multiple delivery methods.

Comment:
Example: Partial cash refund + partial account credit or voucher.
